content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA, which is a permutation of the integers [1,N][1, N][1,N].

Your task is to sort the array using the following operations as many times as you want:


Choose an index iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N), and then pay AiA_iAi‚Äã coins to sort the prefix A[1,i]A[1, i]A[1,i] of the array. (Note that the cost is levied before the sorting operation is performed)
Here, A[1,i]A[1, i]A[1,i] represents the prefix [A1,A2,‚Ä¶,Ai][A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_i][A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Ai‚Äã].

What is the minimum total cost to sort the array?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum cost to sort the array.
constraints: Ai‚âÝAjA_i \ne A_jAi‚ÄãÓÄÝ=Aj‚Äã for all i‚âÝji \ne jiÓÄÝ=j.
